links.text: Crystal Bridges Museum of American Art — Wikipedia

summary: Moshe Safdie is an Israeli-Canadian architect who apprenticed with Louis Kahn. His master's thesis 'A Case for City Living' became Habitat 67, a complex of 354 stacked prefabricated concrete boxes built for Montreal's Expo 67. Other major works include the Marina Bay Sands resort in Singapore (three towers joined by a SkyPark and infinity pool), the Crystal Bridges Museum in Arkansas, and the Kauffmann Center for the Performing Arts in Kansas City.
comprehensive_summary:
Moshe Safdie is an Israeli-Canadian architect who apprenticed with Louis Kahn and developed a career defined by innovative modular design and civic ambition. His master's thesis — 'A Case for City Living' — was submitted to a design contest after professor Sandy Van Ginkel encouraged him, and was developed into Habitat 67: a residential complex of 354 prefabricated 90-ton concrete boxes arranged into 158 apartments, built on a manmade island near the St. Lawrence River for Montreal's Expo 67 in 1967. The complex was guided by the motto 'For Everyone a Garden,' emphasizing terraced gardens above lower units. August Komendant supervised the structural engineering. The original proposal included 1,000 units as well as shopping centers and schools. Jerry Adamson designed special rotary-moulded polyethylene armchairs for the project. Habitat 67 has been compared to Ricardo Bofill's Kafka Castle and inspired the Nakagin Capsule Tower. Safdie proposed extensions of the concept to the Virgin Islands (designed to 'root like a cactus to a rocky peninsula'), to San Juan's Hato Rey neighborhood, and to Golden Dream Bay in Qinhuangdao, China. The book For Everyone a Garden documents the extension to Safdie's native Israel.

The Marina Bay Sands resort in Singapore is another signature Safdie work: three towers whose shapes resemble a deck of cards are connected at their tops by a 340-meter 'SkyPark,' which contains the world's largest infinity pool. The complex appears prominently in Crazy Rich Asians and is documented in the coffee table book Reaching for the Sky.

The Crystal Bridges Museum of American Art in Bentonville, Arkansas was commissioned by Alice Walton (of the Walmart family). Safdie dammed a river to create two pools which divide eight shell-like pavilions of a forested art museum such that two buildings act as bridges over the pond. The museum owns Asher Durand's Kindred Spirits.

The Kauffmann Center for the Performing Arts in Kansas City is designed with symmetrical half-shells of concentric vertical arches and resembles, from the back, a pair of connected sliced-up onions. Safdie's other works include the Yad Vashem Holocaust memorial museum in Jerusalem (opened 2005), the Yitzhak Rabin Center in Tel Aviv (whose White City is home to International Style buildings), the National Gallery of Canada, the Jewel Changi airport terminal in Singapore (a five-story indoor garden with a tropical forest), a chapel on Harvard's campus covered by a pyramidal glass roof over a koi pond, the United States Institute of Peace (white roof resembling a dove's wing), and the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives headquarters.

works.name: Habitat 67
works.indicator: 354 stacked prefabricated concrete boxes; 158 apartments; Montreal Expo 67; St. Lawrence River; 'A Case for City Living'; 'For Everyone a Garden'
works.description: Habitat 67 consists of 354 stacked prefabricated 90-ton concrete boxes forming 158 apartments, built near the St. Lawrence River for Montreal's Expo 67. The design grew from Safdie's master's thesis 'A Case for City Living' (submitted after professor Sandy Van Ginkel encouraged him) and was guided by the motto 'For Everyone a Garden.' August Komendant supervised the structural engineering. The original proposal included 1,000 units. Jerry Adamson designed rotary-moulded polyethylene armchairs for it.

clues.clue: 354 prefabricated 90-ton concrete boxes/cubes forming 158 apartments near the St. Lawrence River
examples: A residential complex in this city consists of 354 identical cubic units arranged and placed together in different orientations
This structure is made up of 354 stacked concrete boxes that form 158 apartments and was built for a 'Man and His World'-themed event along the Saint Lawrence River.

clues.clue: Built for Montreal's Expo 67 in 1967; Expo's motto was 'Man and His World'
examples: designed for the Montreal World Expo
Moshe Safdie used concrete blocks for another of these projects built for Expo 67 in Montreal.

clues.clue: Grew from master's thesis 'A Case for City Living'; motto 'For Everyone a Garden'; each roof provides another unit's garden
examples: whose original proposal also included shopping centers and schools and was outlined in the college thesis 'A Case for City Living.' Interlocking walkways and terraces on top of buildings were part of a plan for this complex, in which each roof provides another unit's garden.

clues.clue: Sandy Van Ginkel convinced Safdie to submit his master's thesis to a design contest; reduced from planned 1,000 units
examples: This building was constructed after Sandy Van Ginkel convinced his student to submit his master's thesis to a design contest, although it was reduced from a planned 1,000 units.

examples: August Komendant supervised the structural engineering of this project

clues.clue: Resembles Ricardo Bofill's Kafka Castle; inspired the Nakagin Capsule Tower
examples: Ricardo Bofill's Kafka Castle strongly resembles this structure, and the construction technique used to design it inspired the similar-looking Nakagin Capsule Tower.

clues.clue: Jerry Adamson designed special rotary-moulded polyethylene armchairs for this structure
examples: Jerry Adamson designed special rotary-moulded polyethylene armchairs made specifically for this structure.

clues.clue: Planned extensions to the Virgin Islands ('root like a cactus to a rocky peninsula'), San Juan's Hato Rey, and China; book For Everyone a Garden documents extension to Israel

The book For Everyone a Garden documents the architect's extension of this project to his native Israel.

works.name: Crystal Bridges Museum of American Art
works.indicator: Bentonville, Arkansas; commissioned by Alice Walton; dammed river creating pools; two buildings as bridges over a pond; owns Kindred Spirits
works.description: The Crystal Bridges Museum in Bentonville, Arkansas was commissioned by Alice Walton (of the Walmart family). Safdie dammed a river to create two pools that divide eight shell-like pavilions, with two buildings serving as bridges over the pond. The museum owns Asher Durand's Kindred Spirits.

clues.clue: Commissioned by Walmart heiress Alice Walton in Bentonville, Arkansas; features arched copper roofs
examples: Bentonville, Arkansas is the home of this art museum that features arched copper roofs. Moshe Safdie designed this museum, which was founded by Walmart heiress Alice Walton.

clues.clue: Dammed a river to create two pools; two buildings serve as bridges over the pond; eight shell-like pavilions in a forested setting
examples: Safdie dammed a river to create two pools which divide eight shell-like pavilions of a forested art museum such that two buildings act as bridges over the pond.

clues.clue: Owns Asher Durand's Kindred Spirits
examples: This Arkansas museum designed by Moshe Safdie owns Asher Durand's landscape painting Kindred Spirits.

He apprenticed with Louis Kahn.
